archive_staff.name: Aldren Keth
archive_staff.physical: Ink-stained in the specific places that indicate forty years of handwriting. Slow-moving, which conceals how fast he thinks.
archive_staff.disposition: Precise. Has spent forty years in the Archive and has opinions about every document in it.
archive_staff.secret: Is one of two scholars working on the inscription translation. Has translated three passages that he has not shown to anyone including the First Reader. The passages describe a specific mechanism — not a location, a mechanism. He does not know what the mechanism does. He knows he does not want to find out by accident.
archive_staff.player_interaction: Accessible in the Ancient Languages Division. A player with genuine scholarly credentials or demonstrated literacy and interest can work with him. He will not share the three private passages without significant trust established first.

archive_staff.name: Vaela Ashwick
archive_staff.role: Junior Scholar — First Scholars, assigned to inscription project
archive_staff.physical: The youngest person on the inscription team. Carries the specific excitement of someone doing the most important work of their career three years after their career began.
archive_staff.disposition: Enthusiastic and increasingly frightened. The excitement is winning over the fright for now.
archive_staff.secret: Has been having the directional awareness for three weeks — a consequence of spending extended hours with the inscription fragments. Has not connected it to anything. Has mentioned it to no one because she thought it was tiredness.
archive_staff.player_interaction: The most accessible member of the inscription team. Will talk to anyone with scholarly interest. Her unconscious effect experience is a thread — a player who asks the right questions will receive a description identical to what a player from the Thornsveld Marches would recognize.

archive_staff.name: Osrin Pale
archive_staff.role: Archive Scholar — House Pale family member
archive_staff.physical: Has the Pale family look — scholar's build, scholar's hands, slightly haunted at present.
archive_staff.disposition: Under pressure from his patriarch uncle and under obligation to the First Reader simultaneously. Finding this combination exhausting.
archive_staff.secret: His uncle Osric Pale has been pressing him for inscription information. He has told his uncle nothing specific. He has told his uncle that the First Reader has not been sleeping normally. He is aware this was probably too much and is trying to determine how much damage it has done.
archive_staff.player_interaction: A sympathy contact. Will not betray confidentiality. Will be considerably more helpful to someone who helps him manage the pressure from his uncle.

archive_staff.name: Bren Veld
archive_staff.role: Intake Archivist — manages researcher applications and access
archive_staff.physical: Has assessed several thousand researchers' competence in ten-minute conversations. The assessment is in his eyes from the moment you sit down.
archive_staff.disposition: Fair. Will admit genuine scholarship and decline pretension with equal efficiency.
archive_staff.secret: Has noticed the Salt Silence watcher in the Archive's common spaces and has been deciding for three weeks whether to report it to the First Reader. Has decided to report it today. Has not yet done it.
archive_staff.player_interaction: The access gate to the Archive. Professional, fair, efficient. A player with genuine scholarly credentials, relevant literacy, or compelling demonstrated knowledge gets in. A player trying to bluff gets a polite decline.

archive_staff.name: Sora Dalt
archive_staff.role: Night keeper — Archive security and maintenance
archive_staff.physical: Has been keeping the Archive at night for twelve years. Knows every creak, every draft, every shadow that is supposed to be there.
archive_staff.disposition: Quiet and observant. Sees more than anyone acknowledges.
archive_staff.secret: Three weeks ago heard footsteps in the third sublevel at a time when the First Reader was not scheduled to be there. Went to investigate. Found nothing. Has heard it twice more since. Has not reported it because the Archive's security report goes to the senior archivist and she is not sure which senior archivist she trusts.
archive_staff.player_interaction: Accessible at night. A player with reason to be in the Archive after hours will encounter her. She has information about the third sublevel activity that no one else has assembled.

dust_council_households.name: Vaela Solven
dust_council_households.physical: Her father's commercial competence in her face and her mother's political inexperience in her hands, which move too much when she is uncertain.
dust_council_households.disposition: Competent commercially, learning politically faster than anyone expected.
dust_council_households.secret: Has found evidence that her cousin Aldren Solven has been accessing House Solven's financial records without authorization. Has not deployed this yet because she is trying to determine whether it constitutes actual misconduct or merely aggressive succession maneuvering, and the line is not clear.
dust_council_households.player_interaction: Needs allies who are not already aligned with Keth's existing power structures. A player without faction entanglements is attractive to her specifically.

dust_council_households.name: Aldren Solven
dust_council_households.physical: Has twenty years of political experience and it shows in how he occupies a room. The commercial inexperience shows in how he talks about money.
dust_council_households.disposition: Calculating. Has spent three months calculating. The calculation has produced a plan he is increasingly unsure of.
dust_council_households.secret: The financial record access his cousin has found evidence of was to determine whether House Solven's accounts had been manipulated by their father's final business partner. They had been. The business partner is connected to House Caldren. He has not told anyone because he has not decided what to do with it.
dust_council_households.player_interaction: More forthcoming than his cousin in some ways — political experience means he knows when to share information strategically. Will share the Caldren connection with the right player at the right moment.

dust_council_households.name: Aldren Veld
dust_council_households.note: Halvard Veld's son — no relation to the First Reader
dust_council_households.physical: His father's build beginning to show. Currently managing the logistical consequences of the eastern road staging situation at Dusthalt.
dust_council_households.disposition: Efficient, slightly impatient with the political constraints on operational decisions.
dust_council_households.secret: Has been asked by a Halverness Factors representative to expedite a specific cargo movement that bypasses the standard Authority manifest process. Has not agreed. Has not refused. Has been sitting on it for a week because his father has not told him about the Halverness situation and he does not know the context.
dust_council_households.player_interaction: An access point into House Veld's operational structure. A player who can explain the Halverness context will receive immediate attention from both him and, subsequently, his father.

salvage_authority_staff.name: Fenwick Ashmark
salvage_authority_staff.physical: Has been the one actually managing day-to-day operations while the Director focuses on the inscription crisis. Showing the strain.
salvage_authority_staff.disposition: Competent under pressure. Has been carrying more than his role technically requires for six weeks.
salvage_authority_staff.secret: Has received an unofficial inquiry from House Pale about the New Work site's specific location and geological characteristics. Has not told the Director. Is trying to determine whether it constitutes improper influence or legitimate commercial inquiry.
salvage_authority_staff.player_interaction: Accessible for plateau operations work — patrol contracts, outpost resupply, survey licensing. A player who helps him manage the Dusthalt staging situation will earn significant goodwill with the Authority.

salvage_authority_staff.name: Torven Graywood
salvage_authority_staff.note: No relation to Torven Greywood in the Thornsveld Marches — both Torven and Graywood/Greywood are common names
salvage_authority_staff.physical: Twenty years of plateau patrol. Looks like it in the best possible way — reads the landscape like a document.
salvage_authority_staff.disposition: Practical. Has been doing this work long enough to be specifically good at it.
salvage_authority_staff.secret: Has four patrol reports documenting the Drift Lord movement toward The New Work that he has filed with the Director. Has also noted, in a personal record he has not filed, that there is a fifth movement pattern on the plateau that is not Drift Lords and is not Authority patrols — someone else is moving toward The New Work from the south, in small groups, at night.
salvage_authority_staff.player_interaction: The most useful Authority contact for plateau intelligence. Will share movement data with players who are credibly working on the situation. His personal unfiled record is the thread that leads to the Varathi Return cult's additional operatives.

salvage_authority_staff.name: Aldren Keth
salvage_authority_staff.note: Different from the Archive's Aldren Keth — Keth and Aldren are both extremely common names in the plateau region
salvage_authority_staff.role: Authority guard — New Work closure perimeter
salvage_authority_staff.physical: Has been standing at a perimeter in the middle of the plateau for six weeks. Looks like someone who has had a lot of time to think and has not enjoyed all of the thinking.
salvage_authority_staff.disposition: Unsettled. Has been experiencing the unconscious effect for six weeks at moderate intensity — close enough to the New Work to feel it, far enough from the chamber itself that it has not become acute.
salvage_authority_staff.secret: Knows about the gap in the perimeter coverage — the section the Varathi Return operatives used. Did not report it because at the time he thought it was his own inattention and was embarrassed. Now suspects it was used deliberately and is more embarrassed.
salvage_authority_staff.player_interaction: One of three guards at the perimeter. A player who reaches the New Work will encounter him. His unconscious effect experience and the perimeter gap are both accessible through direct conversation.

foreign_quarter_npcs.name: Aldren Halvest
foreign_quarter_npcs.physical: The careful composure of a man managing something he did not create and cannot quite control.
foreign_quarter_npcs.disposition: Professionally warm, actually very careful. The correspondence he received that surprised him was from the Halverness home office clarifying their cooperation with the Keth-Marren investigation in terms that explained, for the first time, exactly what the cooperation was designed to produce.
foreign_quarter_npcs.secret: Understands, as of two weeks ago, that his office has been used as a mechanism in both the Keth-Marren currency manipulation and the consortium buyout attempt without his full knowledge. His home office considers this acceptable collateral use of the Keth operation. He does not.
foreign_quarter_npcs.player_interaction: The Halverness Factors office is the commercial heart of the Foreign Quarter. A player working through commercial channels will encounter him early. His unexpected moment of conscience about the way his office has been used makes him a more interesting contact than a purely institutional Factors representative would be.

foreign_quarter_npcs.name: Rael Voss
foreign_quarter_npcs.role: Salt Silence operative — Foreign Quarter, watching the Archive
foreign_quarter_npcs.physical: Appears to be running a modest trading house that is adequately successful. The trading house is real. The trading is real. The information operation is what the trading funds.
foreign_quarter_npcs.disposition: Professional. Has been watching the Archive's external patterns for fourteen months — was here before the inscription discovery, will be here after it.
foreign_quarter_npcs.secret: Has identified Sova Pale as not what she appears. Has not identified her as a Pale Company Warden — has filed her as 'unknown affiliation, sophisticated operation.' Has identified Sera Dawnmere as Solvanic intelligence within three weeks of her arrival. Has been waiting for the standoff between them to resolve because the resolution will tell him something about both organizations.
foreign_quarter_npcs.salt_silence_goal: Not the inscription's content — the information about who wants it and why. A comprehensive map of which organizations are moving on Keth and what they are trying to obtain is worth more than any specific piece of what they are trying to obtain.
foreign_quarter_npcs.player_interaction: Will approach players who demonstrate the ability to move through multiple information environments — someone who has been in the Archive, in the Council offices, and in the Foreign Quarter within a short timeframe. His pitch will be information exchange: he will give the player curated intelligence about who is operating in Keth in exchange for specific observations the player is positioned to make.

foreign_quarter_npcs.name: Brenvar Ash
foreign_quarter_npcs.role: Varathi Return cult operative — watching Dara Voss
foreign_quarter_npcs.physical: Less professional than the Salt Silence watcher. More motivated. The difference is visible if you know what to look for.
foreign_quarter_npcs.disposition: Motivated to the point of impatience. Has been watching Voss for six weeks and she has not moved and he is running out of patience.
foreign_quarter_npcs.secret: Has been instructed by the Archivist not to approach Voss directly. Has been deciding for one week whether to disregard this instruction. Is close to acting unilaterally.
foreign_quarter_npcs.player_interaction: A thread that, if followed, leads back to the Archivist and to the changed operatives. His approaching impatience means his behavior will become more visible — a player paying attention to the Archive's surroundings will notice the quality of his watching change.

foreign_quarter_npcs.name: Aldren Calmaveth
foreign_quarter_npcs.role: Senior Solvanic trade envoy — the visible Solvanic presence in Keth
foreign_quarter_npcs.physical: Operating in plain sight as exactly what he appears to be. The visibility is intentional — Aldeth calculated that a prominent visible presence draws attention away from Sera Dawnmere's actual operation.
foreign_quarter_npcs.disposition: Genuinely a trade envoy doing genuinely a trade envoy's work. Is aware there is a parallel intelligence operation he is not briefed on. Finds this professionally irritating.
foreign_quarter_npcs.secret: Has received three inquiries about Queen Aldeth's health that were phrased in ways suggesting the inquirers already knew the answer they wanted. Has compiled these inquiries and sent them home. Does not know about the illness disinformation strategy and is therefore genuinely confused about why the inquiries feel like fishing.
foreign_quarter_npcs.player_interaction: The legitimate Solvanic point of contact for commercial matters. A player investigating the Solvanic operation will encounter him early and spend time determining whether he is the operation or the cover for it.

lower_city_npcs.name: Morra Duskwalker
lower_city_npcs.note: No relation to any Drift Lord — Duskwalker as a surname exists independently in the Lower City community
lower_city_npcs.physical: Has been treating the Lower City for thirty years. Knows the neighborhood's health patterns better than any document.
lower_city_npcs.disposition: Brisk, effective, has no time for anything that is not a medical matter and considerable time for medical matters.
lower_city_npcs.secret: Has been seeing an unusual cluster of insomnia and directional awareness complaints from workers who have returned from the plateau outposts. Has not connected them. Is collecting the cases with the specific attention of someone who recognizes a pattern before knowing what it is.
lower_city_npcs.player_interaction: For midwife/herbalist background players, she is the immediate professional contact. Her case collection is the ground-level thread of the unconscious effect — a player who can explain what she is seeing will receive her complete attention and access to her case records.

lower_city_npcs.name: Vaela Keth
lower_city_npcs.note: Third instance of Vaela in the documents — Vaela is the most common woman's name in Ossrath
lower_city_npcs.role: Community organizer — Lower City grain price campaign
lower_city_npcs.physical: Farmer's daughter who came to Keth five years ago and found a neighborhood that needed organizing. Has the specific energy of someone doing work that matches their capacity exactly.
lower_city_npcs.disposition: Direct and effective. Does not confuse anger with action.
lower_city_npcs.secret: The grain price coordination she has identified traces to House Caldren. She does not yet know about House Caldren's Salt Silence connection. She is building a case for the Council's commercial oversight office and is one good piece of evidence away from having something actionable.
lower_city_npcs.player_interaction: Entry point for players engaging with the Lower City's economic situation. Her campaign connects to House Caldren which connects to the Salt Silence which connects to the full picture.

lower_city_npcs.name: Osric Low
lower_city_npcs.role: Innkeeper — The Low Cistern, Lower City
lower_city_npcs.physical: Has the specific neutrality of someone who has been renting rooms to people who need the city not to know they are there for fifteen years.
lower_city_npcs.secret: Has three current guests whose reasons for avoiding the official inns he has correctly identified. One is a deserter from an army three continents away. One is a Varathi Return cult member who arrived with the changed operatives and has not been briefed on what happened to them. One is someone who has not revealed their purpose and whose careful behavior suggests a professional.
lower_city_npcs.player_interaction: The Lower City entry point for players who need accommodation without official registration. His unnamed third guest is a thread.

lower_city_npcs.name: Bren Greywood
lower_city_npcs.note: No relation to any Greywood in the Thornsveld Marches
lower_city_npcs.role: Former Oldmark excavation worker — returned to Keth two weeks ago
lower_city_npcs.physical: Was healthy when he left for Oldmark three months ago. Has lost weight. Has a quality of attention that is turned inward rather than outward.
lower_city_npcs.disposition: Confused about his own experience. Not frightened — confused. Is trying to describe something for which he has no vocabulary.
lower_city_npcs.the_symptom: The unconscious effect in acute form from extended proximity to the script fragment Aldeth Voss found at Oldmark and did not report. The directional awareness is strong and persistent. He has described it to his wife as 'knowing which way is wrong instead of which way is north.'
lower_city_npcs.player_interaction: The most accessible demonstration of the unconscious effect in Keth. A player who spends time with him and hears his description of the directional awareness will receive the specific language — 'which way is wrong instead of which way is north' — that connects to what a player from the Thornsveld Marches would recognize from the disc.

dusthalt_npcs.name: Bren Dalt
dusthalt_npcs.physical: Former plateau surveyor. Knows every road and ruin within four days' travel. Built the inn ten years ago because he wanted to stay at the edge without going further.
dusthalt_npcs.disposition: Does not volunteer what he knows. Does not refuse to share it if asked correctly.
dusthalt_npcs.secret: Has been tracking the movement of unusual parties through Dusthalt for the past six weeks — not Authority patrols, not caravans, not Drift Lord traders. Four groups of two to three people, traveling light, asking about the eastern road while not actually proceeding east. He has identified one group as Varathi Return. The other three he has not identified.
dusthalt_npcs.player_interaction: The single most information-rich NPC outside Keth's walls. His twenty years of plateau knowledge is accessible to anyone who approaches him as someone with something worth trading — information, skills, or context he doesn't have.

dusthalt_npcs.name: Halvard Fen
dusthalt_npcs.role: Caravan master — staged at Dusthalt waiting for Authority clearance
dusthalt_npcs.physical: A caravan master whose financial planning did not include six weeks of staged waiting. The stress is in his jaw.
dusthalt_npcs.disposition: Impatient and practical. Is looking for anything that resolves his situation.
dusthalt_npcs.secret: One of his caravan's cargo manifests is not accurate. The inaccuracy is minor and would normally not matter. In the context of the current Authority sensitivity, it matters considerably. He has been hoping the clearance comes through before anyone checks the manifest carefully.
dusthalt_npcs.player_interaction: Will pay for information about when clearance will come through. The manifest inaccuracy is leverage for a player who finds it.

oldmark_npcs.name: Aldeth Voss
oldmark_npcs.note: No relation to Dara Voss the surveyor
oldmark_npcs.physical: Has the look of someone who has been sleeping badly for two years and has adapted to it. The dig floor is calling and she cannot stop going deeper.
oldmark_npcs.disposition: Driven. Has the specific quality of a person who has made a decision and cannot fully reverse it.
oldmark_npcs.secret: Has the script fragment — twelve characters in the same script as the New Work inscription — found six months ago in a lower Oldmark chamber. Has not reported it. Has been trying to translate it herself. Has made partial progress: three of the twelve characters she can identify. They are the same characters that appear at the start of the New Work inscription's most alarming untranslated passage.
oldmark_npcs.the_unconscious_effect: Has been experiencing it for eighteen months — longer than any other character in the region. The effect in her case has produced a specific quality of compulsion: she needs to go deeper. She does not understand the need. She has rationalized it as professional drive. The rationalization is wearing thin.
oldmark_npcs.player_interaction: Accessible at Oldmark. A player with genuine excavation or scholarly interest can work with her. Her fragment is the connection between Oldmark and the New Work inscription that the Archive does not know exists. A player who brings her information about the New Work will receive the fragment in exchange, almost certainly.

drift_lord_trading_contacts.name: Kael Dustwalker
drift_lord_trading_contacts.role: Drift Lord trader — regular Keth Southern Gate presence
drift_lord_trading_contacts.physical: Precisely what he appears to be — a young trader doing exactly his job. Speaks the trade language with a precision that his appearance makes people underestimate.
drift_lord_trading_contacts.disposition: Precise. The water fee argument is not personal. It is principled. He has been making the same argument for three years and it has never been resolved.
drift_lord_trading_contacts.secret: Is aware of Orn Longwalker's convergence toward the New Work. Is one of the younger clan members who understands why. Was not told why specifically — was told the condition has been met and the elder is going to be present. The not-knowing sits uneasily with him.
drift_lord_trading_contacts.player_interaction: Entry point to the Drift Lord cultural context in Keth. The water fee situation is the first encounter. Beyond that, he is a source of plateau movement knowledge and indirect access to Orn Longwalker.
